reforms.description: Costa Rica plans to strengthen public research funding by using competitive and performance-based criteria, along with establishing independent evaluation mechanisms to enhance innovation outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public research funding is expected to stimulate innovation and improve interactions between universities and the business sector, leading to higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Plans to fund public research using competitive criteria have been outlined.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public research is funded, aiming to enhance the effectiveness of innovation policies and improve the overall research landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Fund public research using competitive and performance-based criteria and establish independent evaluation mechanisms.

reforms.description: The Development Banking System (SDB) is set to shift its resources and priorities towards supporting SMEs' innovation and entrepreneurship, enhancing funding for training in risk capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing on innovation and entrepreneurship, the SDB aims to improve access to credit for SMEs, which is crucial for their growth and integration into global value chains.
reforms.status_evidence: Plans to shift SDB resources towards supporting SMEs innovation have been outlined.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major shift in the focus of the SDB, aiming to significantly enhance the support for SMEs, which are vital for economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Shift SDB resources and priorities towards supporting SMEs innovation and entrepreneurship.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is implementing a comprehensive education reform to address skills shortages in high-tech and advanced sectors, which are critical for attracting foreign direct investment and maximizing trade benefits.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the education system will build a skilled workforce, which is essential for innovation and attracting high-tech industries.
reforms.status_evidence: A comprehensive education reform is underway to address skills shortages.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses critical skills shortages that threaten the country's economic competitiveness and ability to attract foreign investment.
reforms.source_quote: A comprehensive education reform is underway, but key timelines and milestones are still unclear.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is enhancing trade facilitation efforts to improve integration into global and regional value chains, which is expected to boost innovation and competitiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved trade facilitation will lower costs and enhance access to international markets, fostering innovation among local firms.
reforms.status_evidence: Trade facilitation efforts have regained considerable impetus since 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for enhancing trade competitiveness but is part of ongoing efforts rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Trade facilitation efforts have regained considerable impetus since 2022.

reforms.description: Costa Rica has recently established a national strategy for Artificial Intelligence to integrate AI efficiently into public administration, aiming to enhance government processes and service delivery.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Integrating AI into public administration can improve efficiency and service delivery, potentially leading to better resource allocation and enhanced public services.
reforms.status_evidence: The establishment of the national AI strategy has been publicly announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: The national AI strategy represents a significant shift in how Costa Rica plans to utilize technology in governance, with potential long-term impacts on efficiency and service delivery.
reforms.source_quote: Costa Rica has recently established an Artificial Intelligence national strategy to integrate Artificial Intelligence efficiently in the public administration.

reforms.description: Costa Rica has established an Investment Single Window Facility to streamline the administrative requirements for setting up formal enterprises, initially targeting free trade zones but now available to all firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying the process of starting a business, the reform encourages formalization, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Investment Single Window Facility is now operational for all firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards reducing bureaucratic barriers for business creation, which is crucial for enhancing the entrepreneurial ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Costa Rica has taken steps in this direction with the establishment of an Investment Single Window Facility.

reforms.description: The 'We Let You Work' programme aims to identify and remove bureaucratic hurdles that slow down business operations, having resolved 140 out of 163 identified bottlenecks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing bureaucratic obstacles, the programme facilitates smoother business operations, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has resolved 140 bottlenecks in business operations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This programme represents a significant effort to improve the business environment by reducing regulatory burdens, which is essential for fostering entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry worked with the private sector... to pinpoint key obstacles.

reforms.description: A 2023 law established new incentives aimed at attracting foreign direct investment (FDI) outside the Great Metropolitan area, targeting companies in export activities and sectors such as health, agriculture, and sustainable parks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The incentives are expected to diversify the sources of FDI, enhancing economic resilience and promoting innovation in targeted sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was enacted to attract new FDI projects, with 59 new projects reported since its implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in FDI strategy, aiming to enhance economic diversification and resilience by targeting new sectors and regions.
reforms.source_quote: A 2023 law established new incentives aimed to attract FDI outside the GMA targeting companies in export activities as well as in areas such as health, agriculture and sustainable parks.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is enhancing its National Plan for Science and Technology (NPST) to focus more on innovation outputs, addressing the country's low performance in producing and diffusing new knowledge.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By prioritizing innovation outputs, the reform aims to increase productivity and competitiveness in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The NPST is undergoing an assessment review launched in June 2024 to enhance its focus on innovation outputs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the national approach to science and technology, aiming to improve innovation performance and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The NPST not focusing on innovation outputs, an aspect that could be considered in the assessment review of the NPST launched in June 2024.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is developing VUCE 3.0, a digital platform aimed at streamlining trade processes for SMEs, which will enhance their ability to engage in international trade.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying trade processes, the reform is expected to lower barriers for SMEs, enhancing their competitiveness and integration into global markets.
reforms.status_evidence: The ongoing development of the VUCE 3.0, to be completed by 2027, provides an opportunity to integrate improvements targeting exporting SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant improvement in trade facilitation for SMEs, which is crucial for their growth and internationalization.
reforms.source_quote: The ongoing development of the VUCE 3.0, to be completed by 2027, provides an opportunity to integrate improvements targeting exporting SMEs.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is designing a long-term national innovation strategy to identify key technologies and sectors for growth, involving stakeholders from government, academia, and the business sector to ensure continuity and resilience against political changes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning innovation efforts with strategic priorities, the strategy aims to enhance productivity and foster a more robust innovation ecosystem.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy is in the design phase and aims to involve all relevant stakeholders.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in how innovation policy is approached in Costa Rica, aiming for a coordinated and strategic framework that can significantly impact economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Designing a long-term national innovation strategy would help identify technologies and sectors with strategic importance in terms of growth and employment opportunities.

reforms.description: The Development Banking System (SDB) has undergone reforms to enhance its role in supporting SMEs' innovation and technology transfer, including the introduction of new tools like seed capital and capital risk instruments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing access to funding for innovation, these reforms aim to stimulate entrepreneurial activity and enhance the capacity of SMEs to innovate.
reforms.status_evidence: The SDB has started distributing seed capital via ten agencies targeting different activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms significantly enhance the funding landscape for SMEs, which is crucial for fostering innovation and scaling up businesses.
reforms.source_quote: Reforms in 2014 and 2021, together with the issuance of new regulation in 2023, attempted to enhance the role of the SDB in supporting SMEs innovation.

reforms.description: The SDB aims to promote alternative sources of funding for innovation through seed capital for early-stage startups and capital risk tools for late-stage startups, focusing on areas with high potential for boosting SMEs' business innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing targeted funding to startups, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and entrepreneurship, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The SDB has outlined plans to promote funding for innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance funding mechanisms for startups, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The SDB should continue promoting alternative sources of funding for innovation via seed capital for early-stage startups.

reforms.description: The National System of Technical Vocational Education and Training (SINEFOTEP) was created to improve the quality and effectiveness of vocational education and training programs in Costa Rica.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing vocational training, the reform aims to equip the workforce with necessary skills, thereby improving employability and supporting innovation in high-demand sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The creation of SINEFOTEP was announced as a key initiative to improve vocational education.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses the critical need for skilled labor in the economy, particularly in STEM fields, which is essential for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The creation of the National System of Technical Vocational Education and Training (SINEFOTEP) in April 2024 could improve monitoring the quality and effectiveness of VET programmes.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is enhancing its digital skills training through a public-private cooperation aimed at creating a training facility for the semiconductor industry, focusing on advanced digital skills such as AI and cybersecurity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to build a skilled workforce in high-demand sectors, which is essential for attracting foreign investment and fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been publicly announced and is set to begin implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards addressing the skills gap in the technology sector, which is crucial for economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Initiatives aimed to strengthen the supply of specialised courses in high-demand advanced digital skills areas include a public-private cooperation among the National Apprenticeship Institute (INA) and the private sector.

reforms.description: Costa Rica initiated an open tendering process to assign 5G spectrum frequencies to telecommunication operators, aimed at enhancing connectivity and supporting digital infrastructure development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The deployment of 5G networks is expected to improve connectivity, which can enhance productivity and innovation across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: An open tendering process for 5G spectrum frequencies started in the summer of 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tendering process represents a significant step towards improving digital infrastructure, which is crucial for economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Costa Rica started in the summer of 2024 an open tendering to assign the 5G spectrum frequencies to telecommunication operators.
